steps.title: Identify the problem the blank must follow from
steps.content:
The passage establishes one specific problem: it is challenging to accurately count these primates, so researchers cannot tell whether the population is increasing, decreasing, or stable. The blank must complete “The study may thus ______” — the completion must follow logically from this counting difficulty.
  • Choice A — 'risk making inaccurate conclusions about the effectiveness of different conservation strategies.': If population changes cannot be measured accurately, any conclusions about which conservation strategy is working will also be unreliable. Follows directly from the counting problem. ✓
  • Choice B — 'cause other conservationists to adopt a new methodology for counting populations.': The passage gives no indication that other conservationists will change their methods as a result. ✗
  • Choice C — 'benefit from including species beyond the white-headed langur.': Expanding the study’s scope does not address the counting difficulty described. ✗
  • Choice D — 'fail to consider less-well-known conservation approaches for the white-headed langur.': The passage does not suggest any overlooked conservation approaches. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and conclude
steps.content: Only choice A logically connects the counting difficulty to a consequence for the study’s conclusions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
